messages.text: Waiting on materials from WH to perform the changeover.
QA requested to perform level 2 clean on MFM before we can do the changeover. Rich Brierley

Rose Smuin Rich Brierley 


Chocolate caramel cup appearance with the modifications on the fin in L2 no silver exposed. Should improve performance drastically
Change over ready
Mfm checklist
Mfm centerline
We start the line at 9:15

Good morning the JOBS for MFM are:
40667
40681
Everyone

During the production run, all evaluated parameters remained within established specifications. Product weights were compliant, and the count of 34 pieces per bag was verified. Package seals were found to be acceptable, and production and expiration dates matched across the packaging (bags), cases, and the final pallet.


Additionally, the components required by the specification were verified. No product accumulation was observed in the X-ray unit, and the line flow remained stable and free of deviations throughout the operation.

A visual inspection of the MFM line was conducted due to the change in production date from 07/10/2027 to 07/13/2027. During the verification, it was confirmed that the area and equipment were properly organized and in operational condition, leaving the line ready to begin production for batch 26194.
The line was sanitized by the night shift
Good morning, the line started at 7:15 am
Please post when caramel is at the guillotine
Good morning , the CL of sollich 4
Here is the meter
Theegarten's sollich line 4 was sanitized by the night shift
Good morning team, the first batch reached 14.4%. The next batch is ready to be downloaded to the reserve.
We are already running, we started at 7:58 am.
Good morning,The wrap is great
Caramel weight.

Good morning.




The candy is well-centered, and the paper's cut lines remain uniform.
Erandy Garcilazo Ceja, the replacement should fall under warranty.   I see in the Theegarten support chat there has been some information sent.  Have they provided support to help with the issue?

Jorge Carrillo
Yes, we have reached out and are waiting for further feedback from Theegarten.
I am pretty sure this was already discussed, but can we please update the spec for Hawaiian Host to reflect target fill of 400 lbs instead of 480. Candon Watt Curt Willis
I’ll submit a spec revision request.
Thank you
A gluten-free test was performed on Bequet caramel, and no gluten was detected. Charity Young, Mary Montilla
Italu Martinez, we are running Hawaiian host tomorrow on S4. Can you get us the recipe for 1,000lbs batches please?
The weights were verified, and all are within specifications. The length and height of the candy also meet the established requirements.
Thank you!
This has been sent.
Good morning,The wrap is great
24.15 pounds of packaging material (wrap) are being placed on hold due to a defect: the roll is improperly wound, causing issues with the machines.
Here is the meter

Both kettles are full as well as the reserve on S4. We’ve stopped cooking, and will run out what’s left and then stop the line. 
Dakota Adair Curt Willis
The wrap is good
The wrap is good
Something right here is leaking by tunnel 1 Conner Boyd
Condensation.
Here is the meter
Rafael Baretta we do not have spec for Hawaiian Host. We are running this morning and we need it ASAP, please
Katya Farnsworth Mary Montilla I just emailed you the current Rev of spec. I’ll see how soon we can get you the new one.
I’m working on this right now
Katya Farnsworth this is in Safety Chain now.
Thank you!

Hi Team,
We noticed that a flexible hose had been installed to transfer the corn syrup. It was leaking, possibly due to the temperature or because the connection was not tight enough.
We removed the flexible hose and replaced it with a heavy-duty hose, the same type recommended and used by the Chocotech manufacturer.
Before installing the new hose, Jason from Maintenance Jason Jensen helped us install the fitting. We then cleaned and sanitized it, and QA performed an ATP test. After it passed the ATP test, we installed the new hose and put it into service.
MM4 was creating sealed empty pouches. I updated the cam settings and product is no longer dropping when a pouch doesn't open.
177 bags of scrap for 11: am
Weight checks - 11:00am.
Nut targets and chocolate targets don't look right. Can we please take a look at this? Curt Willis Candon Watt its for the FAD pecan clusters

MM3 and MM4 checkweigher reject bins are full, and both overweight and underweight products are passing through. I have already informed the operator to correct this issue by keeping the reject bins empty.
Charity Young, Katya Farnsworth, Mary Montilla
Mariela Gudino We are pausing the line to perform a CIL on the MM's. We will use that time to clear everything out as well
There is spilled water on the floor under tunnel 2 Katya Farnsworth, Charity Young
We will take care of it
As of 12:25pm we have 4,410 cases left to make to complete FAD job. Which is 24.5 pallets. Justin Garner
This is the condition of the metal detector rollers at 12:30 PM.

We counted how long it takes to consume a full pallet of Pecan (10024) from start to end. It took 3 hours and 13 minutes for the pallet to be completely used. Curt, Justin.


 (DT was subtracted)
Weight checks - 1:00pm.

The piping system that goes from the turbotemper machine to the Enrober has started to drop water, most likely due to condensation. I will place some wet floor signs around the area. All temps are keeping up with no issues. Katya, Justin.
The infeed drive belts weren't moving on MM3. Maintenance discovered that the forks that have to be pressed forward to pick up a pouch were stuck. They freed them up and now it's working.
We have 18.5 pallets left to make of FAD order Justin Garner Approximately 3.5 hrs of run time left
Weight checks - 2:00pm.
Enrober sos
Depositor SoS
MD rollers
Good

We've currently produced just over 10 pallets. So we still need to produce another 6 pallets, and we currently have ~33 bins of accumulation. We've just reduced the line speed form 40 to 39. At this rate, we expect to complete the order around 7:30pm. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
MM4 isn't opening the pouches. Caleb York is here working on it. We've paused the line in order to pass the accumulated product while MM4 is out of order. Dakota Adair Curt Willis

We stopped depositing caramel at 7:13pm. The order is complete, and the pack area is currently working on passing the remaining accumulation. After that we'll take over cleaning the C2 belt and begin reworking the jars of PRS pecan clusters. The kitchen is doing a caustic clean and the enrober is in reheat. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
We've also noticed that the printers for the trays don't appear to be set up. Are there special instructions for this? Troy Baxter Dakota Adair
Curt Willis you are correct we do not print on these trays
Caramel is ready for depositing. We only need QA to verify the quality. Dakota Adair Delia Zambrano Curt Willis Victor Mejia
Depositing now
We don't have the correct job for the BBD printer. The closest we have says "S2", but we need it to say "S3". Dakota Adair Charity Young
Labeler A or B? A should be editable via the screen, B needs the USB
Can we see if there is a different file that has the correct information if not we will need to have QA change it to S3 on the thumb drive
We are working on that right now
None of the files have what we need. The photo I posted was the closest match. Alfonsina Gil is working on getting us the correct date.
Perfect, if needed so we done have to stopped the line maybe have then set the jars with caps on a pallet until it’s fixed
Enrober EoS
Depositor EoS

Weight =865


Piece=46


Doubles =0


Triples=0


Broken =0
Rollers on MD
Why do we need box printers. They are only used for Macadamia and we're supposed to be running pecan clusters. Has the schedule changed?
The schedule has not changed, we are running MMK pecan clusters.
A noise has started on the jar belt; when maintenance checked it, the large roller had so much chocolate buildup that it was causing the scraper to vibrate. We are going to stop to clean the roller.
Line is back to run
Please coach your operators on emptying out catch pans Luis Falcon
Yup I'll take a look

Yes, some type of barrier would be good.

I think that we might need to add bollards on both sides of the door. I understand that this might restrict the flow of traffic in the area but it would protect the door. German, what do you think?

Good afternoon all, 
 
Rich BrierleyMarcos MontanoAlan Greer
 
I wanted to let yall know Preston Elmer needed to take some time off the rest of this week and will be in touch next week. 
 
While he is out please escalate any immediate concerns to Rose, Shawn, or myself for support.
 
Thank you for all the effort in starting up Lindon 3 with your passion around delivering the best everyday!

We are having a lot of dead bugs around the drinking fountain each day. I have been cleaning the cart and replacing the towels, but is there anything else we can do?
You can record it in the Pest control log and the pest control will take care of that when they come back
In the maintime you can apply Sanitizer, that may help
I re-sanitized the top of the cart and put fresh towels down
Yes

This electric pallet jack in the Sollich3 Pack area had cardboard stuffed inside the battery compartment, which is something I'm coaching the team on avoiding. But, when removing it, I noticed that the knob which secures the battery was broken off. I don't know which EPJ this is, as it's missing the name-plate. Tiffanee Taylor

I want to call out that we do not want to reject any trucks at L1 without escalation 

If any trucks arrive without a scheduled delivery or if the load is unknown please reach out on this chat to Ethan Bean Martin Carlos Kirk Nickel and myself to make sure what it is and do we want to receive it or reject it 

Thank you

Everyone

During the production run, all evaluated parameters remained within established specifications. Product weights were compliant, and the count of 34 pieces per bag was verified. Package seals were found to be acceptable, and production and expiration dates matched across the packaging (bags), cases, and the final pallet.


Additionally, the components required by the specification were verified. No product accumulation was observed in the X-ray unit, and the line flow remained stable and free of deviations throughout the operation.

A visual inspection of the MFM line was conducted due to the change in production date from 07/10/2027 to 07/13/2027. During the verification, it was confirmed that the area and equipment were properly organized and in operational condition, leaving the line ready to begin production for batch 26194.
